finding.recommendation: Keep the five native agent names (`deep-context`, `deep-research`, `deep-review`, `deep-improvement`, `ai-council`) and repoint their internal skill references to the new `deep-loop-workflows` skill. Do not consolidate to a unified `@deep-loop` agent: the existing `ai-council`/`deep-ai-council` asymmetry proves skill renames do not require agent renames, and the five agents carry different permissions, state shapes, artifacts, and dispatch contracts. Treat `.codex/*.toml` as manually maintained derived mirrors guarded by the mirror-sync verifier, not as outputs of a checked-in generator.
key_tradeoffs: Keeping names minimizes blast radius: 15 existing mirrors get body repoints but no file/identity rename, while command agent names and Speckit agent_file refs stay stable.
Unified @deep-loop would reduce visible agent count but forces a behavior-sensitive mode router and union/conditional permissions, threatening the byte-identical per-mode artifact bar.
Codex TOML edits persist in-repo, but must be mirrored from canonical .opencode bodies and verified because the active tooling checks drift rather than regenerating TOML.
risks: Final repoint strings depend on whether deep-loop-workflows preserves legacy subtrees or introduces modes/<mode> paths.
A future out-of-repo Codex mirror generator, if it exists, could overwrite direct TOML edits; no checked-in generator was found.
.codex/config.toml does not visibly register deep-context despite the TOML mirror existing; runtime discovery semantics need a separate check.
open_questions: Is .codex/config.toml intentionally missing deep-context registration, or does Codex discover .codex/agents/*.toml independently?
What exact deep-loop-workflows internal layout will path repoints target: flat legacy subtrees or modes/<mode>?
Is there any out-of-repo Codex mirror generator not present in this checkout?
